0a_baseline.note: PR-AUC primary; ROC-AUC inflated by ~99% true-negative mass; accuracy intentionally not reported.

selected.note: recall>=0.97 not reachable at any threshold on leak-free OOF

0b_recall_operating_point.framing: recall is an operating-point CHOICE; precision/false-alarm cost reported beside it, never recall alone.

PU-learning (bagging, OOB).discarded_reason: EVALUATION LEAKAGE: the 81 positive seeds were scored by a classifier trained on those same seeds (always in-bag), so they are memorised and trivially separable. PR-AUC=1.000 is an artifact, NOT a generalisation estimate. Per integrity rule #4 (no evaluating on training rows) this number is discarded and is NOT counted as an improvement. A leakage-free PU estimate would need held-out positives, which standard PU bagging does not provide.

0c_improvements.improvement_caveat: +0.039 PR-AUC on a SINGLE 10-fold OOF; baseline 5x2 std is ~0.071, so this sits inside the CV noise band. Treat as honest upside pending repeated-CV confirmation, not a locked gain.
0c_improvements.PU_excluded: PU-learning PR-AUC=1.000 discarded as an evaluation artifact (see challengers.PU-learning). It is NOT the best method and NOT an improvement.
0c_improvements.headline_decision: The DEPLOYED/REPORTED BOI headline REMAINS the single sigmoid-calibrated LightGBM at PR-AUC 0.880 +/- 0.071 (5x2 CV) / 0.902 (10-fold OOF) — it keeps SHAP exact and is the defensible submission number. CatBoost (0.941) and the LGB+XGB mean ensemble (0.921) are reported as honest, leakage-safe upside that could be adopted after repeated-CV verification; they are not claimed as the headline.
